claims.claim_text: In 2022, an Air Canada customer was told by the airline's chatbot that a bereavement fare could be claimed retroactively, and a 2024 tribunal decision held Air Canada responsible for that false guidance.
claims.rationale: The underlying incident is real, but the primary tribunal decision is on CanLII, which is off the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Zillow Offers shut down after 2021 write-downs and expected losses totaling hundreds of millions of dollars.
claims.rationale: The official Zillow investor release directly supports the write-down and expected-loss figures, but that host is off the allowlist.

claims.claim_text: The MMLU benchmark spans 57 academic subjects.
claims.rationale: The original MMLU paper is the primary source for the benchmark's scope and subject count.

claims.claim_text: Frontier models now score above 90% on popular benchmarks like MMLU, making them less useful for separating top systems.
claims.rationale: Humanity's Last Exam explicitly motivates itself by noting that popular benchmarks such as MMLU have passed the 90% range.

claims.claim_text: MMLU-Pro expands multiple-choice answers from four to ten and emphasizes more reasoning-heavy questions.
claims.rationale: The MMLU-Pro paper directly describes the ten-option format and the shift toward more challenging reasoning questions.

claims.claim_text: Humanity's Last Exam was introduced as a harder benchmark after earlier evaluation sets began to saturate.
claims.rationale: The HLE paper frames itself as a response to benchmark saturation.

claims.claim_text: Static benchmarks are vulnerable to data contamination when benchmark questions or close variants appear in pre-training data, inflating apparent reasoning performance.
claims.rationale: The cited survey is specifically about benchmark contamination in LLM evaluation and supports this failure mode.

claims.claim_text: The Hugging Face Open LLM Leaderboard was retired on March 13, 2025 after evaluating over 13,000 models, partly to avoid benchmark overfitting and irrelevant hill climbing.
claims.rationale: The retirement discussion gives the date, the 13K-plus figure, and the stated reasons for shutting it down.

claims.claim_text: Chatbot Arena uses crowdsourced pairwise human comparisons in which users vote between anonymous model outputs.
claims.rationale: The pooled MT-Bench and Chatbot Arena source directly covers the Arena methodology.

claims.claim_text: The module presents LLM-as-judge as the standard enterprise practice.
claims.anchor_text: Today, the standard enterprise practice is the LLM-as-judge paradigm.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Today, many enterprise teams use LLM-as-judge approaches as part of their evaluation stack.
claims.rationale: The sentence makes a sweeping industry-norm claim that is broader than the cited sources directly establish.

claims.claim_text: The module uses a fixed 500-millisecond guardrail latency threshold as if it were a generally established cutoff.
claims.anchor_text: if a guardrail takes 500 milliseconds to execute, it severely damages the user experience of a real-time streaming chatbot.
claims.suggested_rewrite: if a guardrail adds noticeable per-request latency, it can visibly damage the user experience of a real-time streaming chatbot.
claims.rationale: The exact 500 ms threshold is presented as fact without an authoritative latency source.

claims.claim_text: In 2018, Amazon scrapped an experimental AI recruiting engine that scored applicants from one to five stars and learned to penalize resumes containing the word "women's" after being trained on a decade of historical resumes.
claims.span_hint: section: Responsible AI: Bias, Fairness and Interpretability
claims.rationale: The Reuters investigation is the primary reporting source for the specific Amazon recruiting-tool details, but Reuters is off the allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Demographic parity evaluates whether groups receive positive outcomes at similar overall rates.
claims.span_hint: section: Equalized Odds vs Demographic Parity
claims.rationale: Fairness Through Awareness is a primary source for statistical parity, which the module is describing in plain language as demographic parity.

further_reading.title: Holistic Evaluation of Language Models
further_reading.why_relevant: Broadens evaluation beyond single benchmarks and helps frame multi-dimensional LLM assessment.

further_reading.title: Judging LLM-as-a-Judge with MT-Bench and Chatbot Arena
further_reading.why_relevant: Covers LLM-as-judge methodology, human-preference benchmarking, and known judge biases.

further_reading.title: OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful companion reading for the module's guardrails, prompt-injection, and safety-stack sections.

notes: Per instructions, quiz items, exercise prompts, code blocks, and diagrams were not seeded. The highest-priority gaps are the Air Canada, Zillow, Amazon, benchmark-history, and unsupported industry-practice/latency sentences.
